play.prisoner.ringPicker.title: Which ring did you hit?
play.prisoner.title: Prisoner
play.rules.blindKiller.elimination.body: When a segment's double-hit tally reaches three, the player with that secret number is eliminated immediately.
play.rules.blindKiller.elimination.title: Elimination
play.rules.blindKiller.overview.body: Everyone is assigned a secret number from 1 to 20. Throw at doubles — each double hit is recorded on that segment. When a segment reaches three double hits, whoever holds that number is out. Last player standing wins.
play.rules.blindKiller.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.blindKiller.secret.body: Only you see your number on your device. Opponents see which segments have hits, not who owns them.
play.rules.blindKiller.secret.title: Your secret number
play.rules.blindKiller.throwing.body: Each turn you throw three darts. Any double adds a hit to that segment's tally. Singles and triples do not count.
play.rules.blindKiller.throwing.title: Throwing
play.rules.bobs27.gameOver.body: If your score reaches zero or below, the challenge ends. Finish all 21 rounds without busting for your final score.
play.rules.bobs27.gameOver.title: Game over
play.rules.bobs27.hit.body: Only the locked double counts on numbered rounds. On the bull round, inner bull only.
play.rules.bobs27.hit.title: Hits
play.rules.bobs27.miss.body: If none of your three darts hit the target, subtract the round value from your score.
play.rules.bobs27.miss.title: Misses
play.rules.bobs27.overview.body: Start at 27 points. Work through doubles 1–20, then inner bull. Each hit adds its value; miss all three and subtract the target value.
play.rules.bobs27.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.followTheLeader.match.body: On your turn, match the exact segment and ring in up to three darts. Singles, doubles, triples, and bulls are distinct targets.
play.rules.followTheLeader.match.title: Matching
play.rules.followTheLeader.overview.body: Players take turns matching a shared target. Miss in three darts and you lose a life. Last player with lives wins.
play.rules.followTheLeader.pass.body: If every active player misses the current target, the player who set it may pass the turn to the next player or throw again to keep setting targets.
play.rules.followTheLeader.pass.title: Pass or rethrow
play.rules.followTheLeader.target.body: The first player throws one dart to set the opening target. After that, anyone who matches with spare darts can set a new target with their remaining darts — the last scoring dart wins.
play.rules.followTheLeader.target.title: Setting the target
play.rules.halveIt.overview.body: Six rounds on segments 20 down to 15. Any multiplier on the target segment scores.
play.rules.halveIt.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.halveIt.round.body: Miss the target segment entirely and your running total is halved (rounded down).
play.rules.halveIt.round.title: Halve rule
play.rules.halveIt.start.body: Everyone begins at 301 (default). Highest total after six rounds wins.
play.rules.halveIt.start.title: Starting score
play.rules.halveIt.winning.body: After every player throws on round six, highest total wins.
play.rules.halveIt.winning.title: Winning
play.rules.loop.overview.body: Loop is Follow the Leader on the wiring of the board. Match the leader's wire target — loops, splits, and standard rings — or lose a life. Last player with lives wins.
play.rules.loop.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.loop.play.body: The first dart sets the target. Followers must hit that exact wire area in up to three darts or lose a life. Match early and spare darts can set a new target.
play.rules.loop.play.title: Play
play.rules.loop.targets.body: A target can be a normal scoring wedge, a wire loop on numbers like 6 or 20, or a split between the digits of 11. Upper and lower loops on the same number are different targets.
play.rules.loop.targets.title: Valid targets
play.rules.loop.wires.body: When your dart could mean more than one wire area, the app asks you to confirm. A dart in the loop of 6 is not the same as the large single 6.
play.rules.loop.wires.title: Loops and splits
play.rules.prisoner.capture.body: Hit the inner board and the dart becomes a prisoner. Anyone who later hits the playable area of that same number captures it and adds it to their dart pool.
play.rules.prisoner.capture.title: Prisoners
play.rules.prisoner.lost.body: Miss outside the doubles ring or bounce out and the dart stays on the board one turn — you throw fewer darts next visit, then recover it.
play.rules.prisoner.lost.title: Lost darts
play.rules.prisoner.overview.body: Race clockwise from 1 to 20 through the outer scoring ring. First to finish wins — but missed darts can become prisoners other players capture for extra throws.
play.rules.prisoner.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.prisoner.progress.body: After 1 comes 18, then 4, 13, and so on clockwise. Only hits in the playable ring (triples through doubles) advance your target.
play.rules.prisoner.progress.title: Progress
play.rules.scam.halves.body: When every segment 20–1 is closed, the half ends. Swap roles and play again. Combined score from both halves decides the winner.
play.rules.scam.halves.title: Halves
play.rules.scam.overview.body: Two roles: Stopper closes segments while Scorer points on what's still open. Play two halves, swap roles, highest combined total wins. Bull is not used.
play.rules.scam.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.scam.scorer.body: Aim at the highest segment still open. Only hits on that segment add points this visit — any multiplier counts.
play.rules.scam.scorer.title: Scorer
play.rules.scam.stopper.body: Throw first each visit and hit segments 20 down to 1. Each segment you hit is closed for the Scorer for the rest of that half.
play.rules.scam.stopper.title: Stopper
play.rules.snooker.breaks.body: Alternate red then colour until you miss or reds are gone. Your break ends on a miss; opponent breaks next.
play.rules.snooker.breaks.title: Breaks
play.rules.snooker.colours.body: After a red, nominate then pot a colour for 2–7 points. Colours return to the table; reds do not.
play.rules.snooker.colours.title: Colours
play.rules.snooker.overview.body: Reds are segments 1–15 (1 point each). After a red, nominate a colour: 16 yellow through bull black. Highest frame total wins.
play.rules.snooker.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.snooker.reds.body: Pot any available red to score 1 point and nominate a colour. Once potted, that red stays off the table.
play.rules.snooker.reds.title: Reds
play.rules.ticTacToe.grid.body: Nine cells: the center is bull; the other eight are specific segments. The exact grid is shown during play.
play.rules.ticTacToe.grid.title: The grid
play.rules.ticTacToe.overview.body: Noughts and crosses on a 3×3 grid of dartboard targets. Hit a cell to claim it. First to three in a row wins.
play.rules.ticTacToe.overview.title: Overview
play.rules.ticTacToe.turns.body: Players alternate visits of three darts. The first hit on an open cell claims it for X or O. Already claimed cells do nothing.
play.rules.ticTacToe.turns.title: Turns
play.rules.ticTacToe.winning.body: Three claimed cells in a row — across, down, or diagonal — wins the game. If all nine cells fill with no line, it's a draw.
play.rules.ticTacToe.winning.title: Winning
play.scam.title: Scam
play.snooker.title: Snooker
play.suddenDeath.title: Sudden Death
play.ticTacToe.pad.hint: Hit a grid target to claim it
play.ticTacToe.title: Tic-Tac-Toe
